domain: AI Agent Self-Correction & Error Recovery Systems

validated_gaps.title: Unified Agent Resilience Middleware

demand_signals.signal: Agentic Reliability Framework (ARF) posted as Show HN — addresses $50K-$250K/incident losses from agent failures. Community engagement shows demand.
Portkey published detailed guide on retries + fallbacks + circuit breakers, indicating customer demand for unified resilience. But only covers LLM API layer, not full agent pipeline.
Article describes 7 patterns (Layered Retry, Circuit Breaker, Watchdog, Degradation Chain, Output Validation, Dead Man's Switch, Audit Trail) — all must be hand-built. High engagement.
'Your AI Agent Is Not Broken. Your Runtime Is.' — argues the runtime layer is the missing piece, not the agent logic.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Strong demand from multiple sources. Multiple developers building custom resilience stacks. No unified solution exists.

validated_gaps.title: Agent Circuit Breaker Library

demand_signals.signal: Feature request: 'circuit breaker on tool failures — after N consecutive tool call failures, automatically disable tools for the session and continue in text-only mode.'
Detailed article on circuit breakers for LLM apps: 'Without a circuit breaker, a flaky external API turns every agent request into a slow failure.'
Tutorial showing manual circuit breaker implementation for AI agents — indicates demand and no off-the-shelf solution.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Clear demand from GitHub issues and blog content. Developers are building custom circuit breakers. No agent-native library exists.

validated_gaps.title: Multi-Agent Error Propagation Prevention

demand_signals.signal: Paper 'Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?' identifies coordination breakdowns (37% of failures) and 17.2x error amplification. Active research area.
Galileo published 'Multi-Agent AI Failure Recovery That Actually Works' — indicating enterprise customer demand.
'Escaping the 17x Error Trap' — high-engagement article diagnosing multi-agent error amplification.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Strong academic and industry signals. Multi-agent systems are proliferating but error boundaries are absent. Active pain point.

validated_gaps.title: Intelligent Error Classification & Routing

demand_signals.signal: SHIELDA: 'Structured Handling of Exceptions in LLM-Driven Agentic Workflows' — academic paper proposing structured exception handling, indicating research gap.
'Why AI Agents Fail Silently' — describes silent failures where agents return confident hallucinated responses with no error signal.
2025 AI Agent Report: leading causes of pilot failures include 'Brittle Connectors' — errors not classified or handled appropriately.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Academic papers and industry reports both identify the lack of intelligent error classification. Moderate but growing demand.

validated_gaps.title: Agent Workflow Checkpoint & Resume for Non-LangGraph Frameworks

demand_signals.signal: Feature request: 'Agent workflow crash continue feature' — directly requesting checkpoint/resume for non-LangGraph framework.
Feature request: 'Scheduled Task Error Recovery' — requesting resume from failure point.
'Workflow stops despite retry configuration' — users frustrated that n8n agent workflows can't resume.
Prefect + Pydantic AI integration for 'agents that resume from failure' — indicates market demand.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Very strong demand. Multiple GitHub feature requests across different frameworks. Users explicitly asking for checkpoint/resume.

validated_gaps.title: Self-Healing Agent Runtime (Production-Ready)

demand_signals.signal: 'Show HN: 4-tier self-healing AI agent (was silently broken for weeks)' — someone built one but it broke silently, proving the concept is wanted but hard to implement.
'Sovereign, Self Healing AI' — HN discussion on self-healing systems.
'How I Built a Self-Healing Home Server with an AI Agent' — real-world implementation interest.
'Lessons from 70+ Production Bugs' — detailed post on building self-healing agents. High engagement.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Strong HN interest with multiple Show HN posts. Self-healing is a hot topic but no production-ready generic solution exists.

validated_gaps.title: Agent Code Self-Repair Beyond Simple Retry

demand_signals.signal: 'Slagent — a self-learning tool for AI coding agents' — monitors sessions, learns from failures. Shows demand for learning from past errors.
'Sometimes Replit Agent stuck in loop when fail to make a feature. As non-coder, I don't know how to debug.' — end user pain.
'Why Agents Get Stuck in Loops' — detailed analysis of loop detection and prevention for coding agents.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Moderate-strong demand. Coding agents getting stuck in loops is a widely reported problem. Slagent shows someone is trying to address this.

validated_gaps.title: Cost-Aware Retry & Recovery Budgeting

demand_signals.signal: 'Power users burn $30-$800/month in API calls with minimal visibility into what drove those costs.'
LangGraph agents can 'loop silently in production for extended periods, consuming significant API costs before issues are noticed.'

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Moderate-strong demand. Cost runaway from retries is a known problem. No tool combines cost tracking with retry budget enforcement.

validated_gaps.title: Framework-Agnostic Error Recovery SDK

demand_signals.signal: 'Tool node errors fail workflow instead of returning error to agent for handling' — framework-specific error handling limitations.
'Is there any way to retry the last tool call or task when LLM call throws error?' — ADK users asking for retry that doesn't exist.
Rate limit retry handling is inefficient — 'restarting the agent from scratch for each error.'

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Strong demand. Users across multiple frameworks (n8n, ADK, Pydantic AI) all asking for the same recovery features independently.

validated_gaps.title: Rollback & Undo for Agent Side Effects

demand_signals.signal: 'How reversible is an agentic mistake?' — March 2026 article exploring rollback challenges. Cites Replit database deletion incident.
Chapter 12 of 'Agentic Design Patterns' describes state rollback as critical: 'a financial agent that partially executed a multi-step transfer must be able to undo completed steps.'

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Strong demand. Real-world incidents (Replit) and design pattern books both highlight this. No production tool exists.

validated_gaps.title: Non-Developer Agent Reliability Configuration

demand_signals.signal: 'Every AI agent platform requires developer-level skills to set up, which is insurmountable for knowledge workers.'
n8n (low-code) users struggling with agent error handling — their retry configs don't work as expected.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Moderate demand. The problem is real but the market (low-code AI agents) is still maturing.

validated_gaps.title: Agent Error Recovery Benchmarking & Chaos Testing

demand_signals.signal: agent-chaos: Open-source chaos engineering tool for AI agents with fault injectors for LLM failures, tool failures, data corruption. Early stage.
Paper: 'Assessing and Enhancing the Robustness of LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems Through Chaos Engineering' — academic validation of the approach.
ReliabilityBench: 'Evaluating LLM Agent Reliability' — standardized benchmark published Jan 2026.
ChaosEater: 'Fully Automating Chaos Engineering with LLMs' — automating the entire chaos engineering cycle.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Growing demand. Multiple academic papers and an early OSS tool (agent-chaos). The market is early but the concept is validated.

validated_gaps.title: Real-Time Reflection & Self-Critique Layer

demand_signals.signal: 'Reflection: A Completion Verification Layer for Autonomous AI Coding Agents' — March 2026 article proposing reflection as a standard layer.
'The Reflection Pattern Guide' — popular guide indicating developer interest in productized reflection.
'AI Trends 2026: Test-Time Reasoning and the Rise of Reflective Agents' — trend piece identifying reflection as a 2026 theme.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Moderate-strong demand. Reflection is a trending pattern but no plug-and-play middleware exists.

validated_gaps.title: Long-Running Agent Heartbeat & Watchdog

demand_signals.signal: 4-tier self-healing agent 'was silently broken for weeks' — the watchdog/heartbeat itself broke silently.
Describes 'Watchdog Timer' and 'Dead Man's Switch' patterns — needed but hand-built.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Moderate demand. Important for production agents running long tasks, but niche compared to other gaps.

validated_gaps.title: Error Recovery Analytics Dashboard

demand_signals.signal: Buyer's guide compares 5+ tools — none offer recovery-specific metrics like MTTR, recovery success rate, cost per recovery.

validated_gaps.validation_summary: Low-moderate demand. Observability tools are close to covering this. Incremental improvement rather than net-new market.
